architecture.summary: The system augments LLM code generation with type-system constraints via prefix automata and a search over inhabitable types. During decoding, the model's token-by-token sampling is filtered by what the type system permits at each position. Type validity is checked against a simply-typed λ-calculus foundation (formalized) and extended to TypeScript (practical). The approach intercepts the LLM's next-token distribution and restricts it to tokens that preserve well-typedness of the partial code string, forming a guided search over type-valid inhabitants.

biblio.title: Type-Constrained Code Generation with Language Models

claims.statement: Our approach reduces compilation errors by more than half

Significantly increases functional correctness in code synthesis, translation, and repair tasks across LLMs of various sizes and model families, including state-of-the-art open-weight models with more than 30B parameters
A type-constrained decoding approach that leverages type systems to guide code generation, forming a sound approach to enforce well-typedness on LLM-generated code
Formalized the approach on a foundational simply-typed language and extended it to TypeScript to demonstrate practicality
Large language models still frequently produce uncompilable output because their next-token inference procedure does not model formal aspects of code
LLMs frequently generate code with typing errors, which are beyond the domain of syntax and generally hard to adequately constrain
